hero_title_accent: Compliance Layer for Digital Asset Businesses
hero_subtitle: SFTY Business connects regulatory rules, onboarding, screening, Travel Rule workflows and audit evidence into one operational system designed to meet MiCA AML compliance requirements.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to align AML workflows with MiCA requirements due to fragmented provider outputs and inconsistent rule application across jurisdictions. This creates manual workload, weak audit trails, and gaps in evidencing regulatory adherence for CASPs operating under evolving MiCA obligations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY unifies provider outputs, regulatory updates, case management and audit evidence into one operational layer that helps CASPs apply MiCA AML controls consistently and demonstrate adherence across jurisdictions.
workflow_title: From regulatory update to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY links jurisdictional regulatory changes to internal policy updates enabling structured review, case creation, decision recording and audit-ready compliance evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific MiCA provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit evidence without replacing existing compliance tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports CASPs applying MiCA AML controls with provider orchestration, audit evidence preservation and enterprise deployment for scalable, defensible compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MiCA compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps CASP compliance teams connect jurisdiction-specific MiCA AML rules, Travel Rule data, screening results and case workflows into one operational compliance layer.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support MiCA AML workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects provider outputs and applies MiCA policies to route reviews, manage cases, track decisions and update internal controls across multiple jurisdictions.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, rule changes, decision logs and supporting evidence to help teams maintain audit-ready workflows for MiCA regulatory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, layering policies and cases without disrupting existing compliance environments.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace MiCA compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above providers and does not replace KYC, KYT, Travel Rule or screening tools but structures their outputs within MiCA AML controls.
final_title: Structure MiCA AML controls into one workflow
final_description: Review how SFTY connects regulatory rules, onboarding, Travel Rule events and audit evidence into a connected MiCA compliance layer.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
